span.p: NO — memory-only access token, no refresh

display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: All devices — user requested or security event

span.p: Is there a refresh token stored server-side or in an HttpOnly cookie?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Call the server-side revoke endpoint first — invalidate the refresh token in the token store

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Clear in-memory access token; skip server revocation — nothing to revoke

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is this a single-device logout or all-device logout?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Revoke this session only; other sessions remain active

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Revoke all token families for this user ID

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Clear the session cookie server-side via Set-Cookie Max-Age=0

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Redirect to login page; confirm logout was server-side
